channel.title: Maths on ftvkyo
channel.description: Recent content in Maths on ftvkyo

item.title: In search of the Visibility Polygon
item.description: I have been building a 2D game for fun recently. Once I implemented a basic lighting system for it, I understood that I want game objects to cast shadows. There are multiple ways to achieve that, and the approach I selected is based on visibility polygons. A frame from the game I am building, with differently colored flames lighting up different areas based on their visibility A visibility polygon represents the area that, for a given arrangement of occluding objects, has a direct line of sight to a certain point (called the query point).

item.title: Procedural generation of organic shapes using Bézier curves
item.description: Preface A while ago I got an idea to create some «organic» shapes for 3D-printing. I learned a bunch of interesting stuff trying to do that. This post captures the essence of my experience. The post starts with an introduction to the tools I am using. Then I present the concept of Bézier curves. I provide a bunch of illustrations to make it more intuitive and fun. After we are familiar with both the tools and the curves, I wrap up by showing how it works in practice.
